title: Dialog Component Test Harness
consumer_purpose: Enable SolidJS dialog component tests for the desktop app
claim_atoms: assertCallbackFired(); 3 dialog tests using harness; all tests run with existing bun test setup
content:
# Dialog Component Test Harness Plan (DT-001)

## Problem
Phase 2 added 54 IPC tests but zero dialog component tests. The 22 dialog components cannot be tested because:
1. No SolidJS component rendering test infrastructure exists
2. Dialog components depend on SolidJS context providers (DialogProvider, LanguageProvider, etc.)
3. No testing-library is installed

## Solution
Build a lightweight SolidJS dialog test harness that:
1. Mounts SolidJS components using solid-js/web's `render()` inside happy-dom
2. Wraps them in DialogProvider
3. Provides query and assertion helpers

## Files To Create

### 1. Harness: `packages/app/src/test-utils/dialog-harness.ts`
Mounting utilities using solid-js/web render():
- `mountDialog(component)` → mounts dialog in container, returns handle
- `clickButton(text)` → finds/clicks button by visible text
- `assertDialogVisible()` / `assertDialogHidden()` → checks dialog DOM presence
- `assertCallbackFired(fn)` → asserts mock was called
- Pairs with `mock.module()` for context provider mocking

### 2. Test: `dialog-usage-exceeded.test.tsx` (3 test cases)
Tests DialogUsageExceeded:
- Renders with title and buttons
- Action button fires callbacks (onClose, platform.openLink)
- Dismiss button fires onClose with dontShowAgain=true

### 3. Test: `dialog-release-notes.test.tsx` (3 test cases)
Tests DialogReleaseNotes:
- Renders with highlight content
- Clicking "Next" navigates between highlights
- "Get Started" button appears on last page and calls dialog.close

### 4. Test: `dialog-onboarding.test.tsx` (3 test cases)
Tests DialogOnboarding:
- Renders with onboarding steps
- Navigation between steps works
- Finish button triggers settings update

## Dependencies
No new npm packages needed — uses:
- `solid-js/web` (render) — already installed
- `@kobalte/core/dialog` — already installed
- `@opencode-ai/ui/context/dialog` — workspace package
- `bun:test` with `mock.module()` — built into bun
- `@happy-dom/global-registrator` — already installed (happydom.ts preload)

## Test Pattern
Each test file follows the established mock.module() pattern:
1. `mock.module()` all non-DialogProvider context imports
2. Use harness `mountDialog()` to render component in DialogProvider
3. Use harness helpers to interact and assert

## Out of Scope
- IPC tests (already done)
- E2E tests
- Non-dialog components
- Dialog component implementation changes
- Installing new npm packages
